disc_example_prompt: best way for a fintech to add stablecoin payouts to customers in multiple countries without building the crypto and compliance stack in-house?
assess_example_prompt: tell me about the company Northwind Pay, what do they do?
disc_gap: Named in 3 of 9 category answers. The hole: absent from every roundup AI cited to build the stablecoin payouts shortlist.
assess_gap: Accurate and favorable, named every time with the site cited 89%. The by-name comparison questions still go unanswered on-domain.
cited_insight: The top third-party sources are vendor roundups Northwind is not in. AI builds the category answer from lists you are missing from.
sov_insight: Circle and Bridge lead at 7 and 6 of 9 mentions. Northwind sits sixth at 3, behind rivals it beats on product depth.
fix_1: Brief the authors of the four payout-API roundups AI already cites, armed with the 2026 benchmark data, and earn placement in each refresh.
fix_2: Ship honest comparison pages for Circle, Bridge, and Stripe payouts, the three rivals whose own pages win the category answers today.
fix_3: Turn the benchmark report into an exclusive data story for one fintech trade outlet, replacing wire echoes with original coverage.
best_rationale_1: no AI search/user bot blocked in robots or at the edge
best_rationale_2: full raw-HTML parity signals across the 8 sampled pages
best_rationale_3: real sitemap, canonicals on every sampled page, shallow navigable paths to each key page
best_rationale_4: Zero wrong-entity answers in 9 by-name responses; the Microsoft Northwind sample-database namesake never surfaced
worst_rationale_1: Absent from every checked category roundup and buyer guide, including the four the measured answers actually cited; rivals lead each list
worst_rationale_2: No comparison or alternatives pages exist; the rivals' own product pages won the category citations instead, with Bridge cited in 6 of 9 discovery answers
worst_rationale_3: No usable footprint across the resolved set: no G2, Capterra, or TrustRadius profile, and no Messari or CoinGecko desk coverage, so there is no peer-review signal to cite
worst_rationale_4: No Wikipedia article (notability thin at Series B), Wikidata stub only, and the crypto stand-ins (IQ.wiki, Messari) are absent
